evidence_quotes.quote: Publishers of these video games can easily decide that a game should no longer run on their own servers and switch it off
"This game is designed to play online with other people and is not an opportunity to play on its own."

paragraphs.text: Ladies and gentlemen, I have heard you gather feedback to ensure fair marketing practices within the digital spectrum. The European Stop Destroing Videogames initiative deals with similar, albeit more specific, themes (there is “only” video games), despite the above, I would like to ask you sincerely to consider how you look like those who have already bought a video game (be it digital or analogue). Publishers of these video games can easily decide that a game should no longer run on their own servers and switch it off (whereas the game itself may become unplayable and although it has already been sold to customers without mentioning what could happen). It is simply unfair, as customers who bought a game should now also have the possession of it when they complete the purchase and receive the game). I can give you an example of a game where I have exactly this concern that it could be switched off. I do not, however, believe that I will continue to take your time. The game in question is the title ‘Plants vs. Zombies Garden Warfare’, published in 2014, from the editor ‘Electronic Arts’. This game is designed to play online with other people and is not an opportunity to play on its own. It is therefore ‘online-only’, which of course is not bad, but it may happen that the publisher chooses not to operate this game anymore, which means that much of the game can no longer be accessed, even if you bought it without a warning about such a shutdown, because it does not have an ‘end-of-live plan’ which, beyond the switch-off of servers, provides the opportunity to continue playing it in the manner envisaged. It is important to mention that this is not an isolated case and that many games have already been destroyed and at least as many games are at risk of being destroyed (there is an index made by people to give an accurate picture. This is called ‘Dead game list’ and can be found on ‘wiki.gg’ (if you have interest and time to look more closely). Finally, I would like to sincerely ask you to protect these buyers from the publishers in such a way that they cannot destroy video games, i.e. make them unplayable. I would like to thank you many times for your time and wish a pleasant day. Yours sincerely, Karl
